new_videos.title: How I run 10 deterministic verification checks before every deploy
new_videos.transcript: Today we cover deterministic verification: pin your script outputs against fixtures, then any silent regression fails CI loud. The trick is golden-fixture diffing — don't check the text content, check the shape. Number of items in a range. Required keys present. Types match. That's it.

new_videos.title: Five ways your agent dies in production and how to monitor each
new_videos.transcript: Silent truncation. Drift. Cost blow-outs. Stale dependencies. And the worst one: your agent succeeds at the wrong thing because your success metric is wrong. For each, you need a probe: log the stop reason, score outputs against a golden set, cap tokens per call, pin versions, instrument the success metric directly.

new_videos.title: Random commentary on the AI scene this week
new_videos.transcript: Just some thoughts on what I've been seeing this week. No real experiment to share.

new_videos.title: Why I run my prompts through a stub-LLM before the real one
new_videos.transcript: Local stub mode for any LLM-using script. Take your prompt template, render with sample inputs, save the rendered template. Then a stub mode reads a saved canonical response. You can run your full pipeline end-to-end with zero API calls. Catches shape regressions, prompt drift, and code bugs separate from model behavior.

new_videos.title: Talk: making LLM scripts CI-testable in 30 lines
new_videos.transcript: Wrap your LLM call in a thin adapter. The adapter has two modes: real and stub. Stub reads JSON from disk. Real hits the API. CI runs stub mode. Production runs real. Same code path otherwise. Costs nothing to test, catches all shape regressions.
